skill.name: agent-tools
skill.description: Use the inference.sh belt CLI to discover and run cloud AI apps for images, video, LLMs, search, 3D generation, and X automation.
skill.summary: Run inference.sh cloud AI apps from Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through the belt CLI.
skill.author: halt-catch-fire

security_audit.summary: The main confirmed risks are remote installer execution through curl piped to sh, external CLI command execution, environment API key handling, and user-local binary installation. Most documentation URLs and shell completion examples are false positives, but the skill also has semantic risks around automatic local file uploads and social account actions. No prompt injection language was found in the reviewed files.

remediation.issue: Remote installer is documented as curl piped directly to sh.
remediation.suggestion: Make manual download, checksum verification, and signature verification the primary install path. Keep pipe-to-shell as discouraged legacy guidance or remove it.

remediation.issue: The skill can upload local files to cloud apps when paths are supplied.
remediation.suggestion: Require explicit user confirmation before uploading local files, and document that sensitive files should not be used as inputs.

remediation.issue: X/Twitter commands can post content or perform account actions.
remediation.suggestion: Require confirmation before posting, messaging, following, liking, or reposting from any connected social account.

remediation.issue: API keys may be supplied through environment variables.
remediation.suggestion: Advise users to use least-privilege keys, avoid logging environment values, and rotate keys after accidental exposure.

finding_verdicts.reason: This points users or agents at a remote installer or downloaded binary. The network access is intentional, but it creates supply-chain exposure because install artifacts are fetched from an external host.

The line instructs users to place an API key in INFSH_API_KEY. This is legitimate authentication guidance, but it still handles sensitive credentials in the environment.
This troubleshooting table only tells users to check INFSH_API_KEY after an invalid key error. It does not read, expose, or transmit a secret.
The skill documents curl piped directly to sh for installing the CLI. This executes remote code without prior inspection and is a real supply-chain execution risk.

semantic_findings.title: Automatic Local File Uploads to Cloud Apps
semantic_findings.description: The skill explains that local file paths are automatically uploaded when supplied as inputs. This can expose user files to external cloud apps if an agent selects an unintended path or uploads sensitive media.
semantic_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly describe automatic uploads of local paths and provide commands with local image, audio, and media inputs. This is a clear data-exposure risk, even though it is an intended feature.

semantic_findings.title: External Social Account Actions
semantic_findings.description: The skill includes commands for posting to X/Twitter and lists actions such as direct messages, follows, likes, and reposts. These actions can affect a connected account and should require explicit user confirmation.
semantic_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill directly documents social posting and related account actions. The risk is business-logic impact rather than malware, so medium severity is appropriate.

content.user_title: Run AI Apps from the Command Line
content.value_statement: Teams need one interface for many cloud AI tools without managing GPU infrastructure. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use the inference.sh belt CLI for generation, search, and automation tasks.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install and authenticate the inference.sh belt CLI.
Runs cloud AI apps with inline JSON or input files.
Discovers public apps by category, search term, or app name.
Submits image, video, LLM, search, 3D, and utility jobs.
Tracks asynchronous task status and saves results.
Uploads local media files when paths are provided as app inputs.
limitations: Requires the belt CLI and an authenticated inference.sh account.
Cloud app availability, pricing, and schemas can change outside the skill.
Local file uploads can expose sensitive files if prompts are not reviewed.
Social automation actions require careful user approval.

use_cases.title: Generate creative assets
use_cases.description: Create images, videos, 3D models, or audio outputs from prompts through hosted AI apps.

use_cases.title: Prototype AI workflows
use_cases.description: Test different hosted models from a terminal workflow before building a dedicated integration.

use_cases.title: Automate model research
use_cases.description: Search the app store, inspect app schemas, run sample inputs, and compare model outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Find an app
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agent-tools to find inference.sh apps for image generation. Show three options and explain what each one does.

prompt_templates.title: Run a simple job
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agent-tools to run an image generation app with this prompt: a product photo of a black travel backpack on a white background.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an input file
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agent-tools to get the sample input for google/veo-3-1-fast, adapt it for a short product video, and run it after I approve the input.

prompt_templates.title: Compare model outputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agent-tools to run the same text prompt through two LLM apps, collect the task results, and summarize quality, latency, and cost considerations.

output_examples.input: Find a hosted app for fast video generation.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists matching inference.sh video apps, notes likely use cases, and recommends checking each app schema before running a job.

output_examples.input: Generate an image from a local reference file.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks for confirmation before uploading the local file, then runs the selected app and returns the result link or task status.

output_examples.input: Check the status of a long video task.
output_examples.output: The assistant queries the task ID, reports whether it is queued, running, failed, or complete, and saves outputs only when requested.

best_practices: Review app schemas before running commands with inline JSON or input files.
Confirm every local file path before uploading it to a cloud app.
Use least-privilege API keys and avoid printing secrets in terminal logs.
anti_patterns: Do not pipe remote installers to a shell without verifying the source and checksum.
Do not upload broad directories or sensitive files as app inputs.
Do not perform social account actions without explicit user approval.

faq.question: What does this skill connect to?
faq.answer: It connects to inference.sh through the belt CLI and runs hosted AI apps from the command line.

faq.question: Does it require an account?
faq.answer: Yes. Users need the belt CLI and an authenticated inference.sh account or API key.

faq.question: Can it use local files?
faq.answer: Yes. The CLI can upload local files when paths are provided as app inputs, so review paths first.

faq.question: Can it run Claude models?
faq.answer: The skill documents OpenRouter and LLM apps, including Claude model examples where available through inference.sh.

faq.question: Is it limited to image generation?
faq.answer: No. It also covers video, LLMs, search, 3D generation, utilities, and selected social automation apps.

faq.question: What approvals should users expect?
faq.answer: Users should approve installs, local file uploads, paid cloud jobs, and any social account action before execution.
